title: Speaking
summary: Oral communication skills for German learners. Practice speaking naturally using reason words and connectors in conversations.

content.title: Conversational Connectors
content.explanation: In spoken German, some words are preferred over others for expressing reasons. While 'deshalb' is standard, native speakers often use 'deswegen' or simply 'weil' with colloquial main-clause word order in fast speech.

examples.de: Ich hab den Bus verpasst, deswegen bin ich zu spät.
examples.en: I missed the bus, that's why I'm late.

content.title: Colloquial 'weil'
content.explanation: In casual spoken German, it is very common to hear 'weil' followed by standard main-clause word order (verb in second position), even though traditional grammar requires verb-final order.

examples.de: Ich kann nicht kommen, weil ich habe keine Zeit.
examples.en: I can't come, because I have no time.

title: Writing
summary: Written communication skills for German learners. Focus on using formal connectors like 'denn' and 'daher' in essays, emails, and reports.

content.title: Formal Connectors
content.explanation: In written German, precision and variety are important. Instead of repeatedly using 'weil' or 'deshalb', use words like 'daher' (hence) and 'denn' (for) to elevate your writing style.

examples.de: Das Wetter war schlecht, daher blieben wir im Hotel.
examples.en: The weather was bad, hence we stayed in the hotel.

content.title: Structuring Arguments
content.explanation: When writing an essay or a formal email, use connectors to logically link your points. Start with a premise and follow with a consequence.

examples.de: Wir haben das Budget überschritten. Deshalb müssen wir Kosten sparen.
examples.en: We exceeded the budget. Therefore, we must save costs.

title: Formal Language
summary: Understanding register in German — when to use formal words like 'daher' and 'denn' versus casual alternatives like 'deswegen'.

content.title: Register Choice
content.explanation: Register refers to the level of formality. German distinguishes clearly between formal/professional contexts and informal/casual ones. Choosing the right connector signals competence and respect.

examples.de: Ich war krank. Daher konnte ich nicht am Meeting teilnehmen.
examples.en: I was sick. Hence, I could not attend the meeting.

content.title: Comparing Formal and Informal
content.explanation: Compare 'daher' (formal) with 'deswegen' (informal). Both mean 'therefore', but they are used in different contexts.

examples.de: Formal: Daher bitten wir um Ihr Verständnis.
examples.en: Formal: Hence, we ask for your understanding.

examples.de: Informal: Deswegen bin ich zu spät.
